dependencies: State-operated systems and subcontractors for member eligibility, enrollment and assignment
Third-party vendors for PBM, medical management and member-related services
Healthcare providers including primary care physicians, specialists, hospitals, behavioral health practitioners
Independent third-party vendors for information systems and networks
risks: Ineffectiveness of state-operated systems and subcontractors could adversely affect business
Third-party vendor failures to meet contractual obligations or comply with laws could expose company to litigation and regulatory action
Inability to maintain relationships with provider networks could materially adversely affect profitability
Inability to integrate and manage information systems effectively could disrupt operations
Failure in or breach of operational or security systems including cyber-attacks could have material adverse effect

regulatory_risks: Changes in federal or state laws or regulations including changes to ACA and government healthcare programs
Reductions or delays in funding for government-sponsored healthcare programs
Changes to eligibility requirements for Medicaid, Medicare, CHIP, LTSS, ABD, Foster Care and Health Insurance Marketplace
Expiration of enhanced advance premium tax credits for Marketplace members without renewal or modification
Significant changes or judicial challenges to the ACA
New laws or regulations or changes in existing laws forcing changes to operations
Premium taxes, assessments and provider pass-through payment requirements by states
Minimum medical loss ratio (MLR) requirements and profit sharing requirements
Performance and quality metrics requirements to maintain contracts or receive full revenue
Minimum statutory capital and financial solvency requirements
State insurance holding company acts restrictions on transactions and dividends
CMS Medicare Advantage Star ratings requirements and potential contract termination for ratings below 3.0 stars for three consecutive years
Risk adjustment payment systems and audit requirements
Encounter data submission requirements and potential penalties for non-compliance
Federal and state fraud, waste and abuse laws including False Claims Act and anti-kickback statutes
HIPAA privacy and security requirements and breach notification requirements
Data privacy and security laws including state laws like California Consumer Privacy Act (CCPA)
Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard compliance
Artificial intelligence and automation regulatory requirements
Pharmacy benefits management regulatory risks and uncertainties
Government contract award, administration and performance requirements
State licensure requirements for HMOs, PPOs, TPAs, pharmacies and healthcare providers
NAIC model laws requiring governance practices and risk-based capital requirements
Knox-Keene tangible net equity (TNE) requirements in California
New York contingent reserve requirements
CMS regulations requiring dual-eligible beneficiaries to receive integrated care through D-SNPs beginning in 2030 with restrictions starting in 2027
